title: Helenium mannii
taxonomy: Helenium mannii is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Helenium, which consists of approximately 40 species of plants commonly known as sneezeweed or false sunflowers. The family Asteraceae is one of the largest and most widespread plant families in the world, with over 23,000 species. Helenium mannii is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification of Helenium mannii reflects its botanical relationships and evolutionary history.

intro: Helenium mannii, also known as Mexican sneezeweed or false sneezeweed, is a perennial plant species native to Mexico, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, offering relief from colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Helenium mannii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Helenium mannii is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is commonly used to treat colds, fever, and respiratory infections.
